availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
availableCommands.name: update-config

availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
availableCommands.name: debug

availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
availableCommands.name: simplify

availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
availableCommands.name: batch

availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
availableCommands.name: loop

availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
availableCommands.name: claude-api

availableCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)
availableCommands.name: clear

availableCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
availableCommands.name: compact

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
availableCommands.name: init

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
availableCommands.name: security-review

availableCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
availableCommands.name: usage

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
availableCommands.name: insights

availableCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
availableCommands.name: goal

availableCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
availableCommands.name: team-onboarding

part.delta: I'll delegate this to two child sessions: one for drafting the improvement plan, one for reviewing it for meas

params.prompt: You are a drafter for a Firegrid coordination-patterns experiment harness. Produce a SHORT improvement plan (max 400 chars) for the harness. Context: harness compares 3 arms (one-participant, central-orchestrator, peer-choreography). Shared board uses channels coordination.{work,claims,findings,questions,reviews,final}. Driver uses Firegrid.channels.send/waitFor; runs are incomplete without a coordination.final row. Risks: agents wasting tokens on unavailable shell/fs tools; optional prose finals defeating scoring; hidden driver state hiding choreography behavior. Output ONLY the plan text, 3-5 bullets, no preamble.

rawInput.prompt: You are a drafter for a Firegrid coordination-patterns experiment harness. Produce a SHORT improvement plan (max 400 chars) for the harness. Context: harness compares 3 arms (one-participant, central-orchestrator, peer-choreography). Shared board uses channels coordination.{work,claims,findings,questions,reviews,final}. Driver uses Firegrid.channels.send/waitFor; runs are incomplete without a coordination.final row. Risks: agents wasting tokens on unavailable shell/fs tools; optional prose finals defeating scoring; hidden driver state hiding choreography behavior. Output ONLY the plan text, 3-5 bullets, no preamble.

params.prompt: You are a reviewer for a Firegrid coordination-patterns experiment harness improvement plan. The plan will propose changes to a harness that compares 3 arms (one-participant, central-orchestrator, peer-choreography) on a shared channel board (coordination.{work,claims,findings,questions,reviews,final}). Without seeing the draft yet, list (max 400 chars, 3-5 bullets) the TOP failure modes and measurement biases a reviewer should check for in ANY such plan: e.g., arm-asymmetric instrumentation, prose-vs-row finals, hidden driver coupling, tool-access drift, scoring leakage. Output ONLY the review checklist, no preamble.

rawInput.prompt: You are a reviewer for a Firegrid coordination-patterns experiment harness improvement plan. The plan will propose changes to a harness that compares 3 arms (one-participant, central-orchestrator, peer-choreography) on a shared channel board (coordination.{work,claims,findings,questions,reviews,final}). Without seeing the draft yet, list (max 400 chars, 3-5 bullets) the TOP failure modes and measurement biases a reviewer should check for in ANY such plan: e.g., arm-asymmetric instrumentation, prose-vs-row finals, hidden driver coupling, tool-access drift, scoring leakage. Output ONLY the review checklist, no preamble.

part.delta: launched. Now I'll wait for the drafter to reply.

part.delta: Got partial draft text. Let me continue collecting to capture the full draft and T
The match filter doesn't actually filter on `_tag`. Let
me keep walking sequences to get reviewer's text content.
I've observed text from both children.

params.payload:
{"kind": "final", "title": "Review-and-Revision: Harness improvement plan (central arm)", "status": "complete", "workId": "review-revision-central", "runId": "2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z", "arm": "central", "body": "Evidence inspected: task packet + replies from two child sessions (drafter, reviewer) via session.agent_output TextChunks.

DRAFT (from drafter child): plan bullets included contract-enforced coordination.final schema/scorer; agents told only Firegrid tools (no shell/fs); pre-flight self-check rejecting prose-only finals; structured per-arm trace of claims/findings/reviews counts for scoring parity.

REVIEW (from reviewer child): top biases to guard: arm-asymmetric instrumentation; tool-access drift (must hold identical channel/tool/permission surface per arm); prose-vs-row final ambiguity; hidden driver coupling leaking choreography state; scoring leakage via tool-allowlist diffs.

REVISED PLAN: (1) Make coordination.final a typed row with required fields {kind,title,body,status,workId,arm}; reject prose-only. (2) Fix identical tool surface per arm (only Firegrid session+channel tools; no shell/fs/exec). (3) Driver observes ONLY channel rows; assert no out-of-band reads. (4) Per-arm counters (claims/findings/reviews/questions) emitted as a meta row. (5) Pre-flight harness self-check that exercises each channel once before the timed run.

Recommendation: ship (1)+(2)+(3) as the minimum viable parity fix; (4)+(5) follow.

Open questions: how to score choreography fairness when peers stay silent; whether to bound child-session count per arm; how to detect a participant that fabricates rows it never acted on.

Coordination effect: delegation produced both a draft and an independent bias checklist before revision; the reviewer's tool-access-drift point reshaped bullet (2) from 'document tools' to 'enforce identical surface'."}
